pattern: When every record is a fixed-width string, the field you actually care about lives at a known, constant character offset, so you can skip any real parsing and just slice out those two characters and compare them numerically.
hints: Every record has the exact same length and layout, so the age is always sitting at the same pair of character positions in every string.
Slice out those two characters, convert them to an integer, and compare against the age threshold.
Count strictly, not inclusively: a record whose age equals the threshold itself should not be counted.

constraints.note: each element is exactly 15 characters: 10 digits, then 'M'/'F'/'O', then a 2-digit age, then 2 more digits

edge_inputs.description: three passengers with mixed ages, two above the threshold
two passengers, neither above the threshold
age exactly at the threshold must not be counted
age one above the threshold must be counted
an age string with a leading zero must still parse as a small number, not be counted
gender marked as the third option 'O' should not affect the age check
several passengers all sitting exactly at the threshold age, none should count
the minimum and maximum possible two-digit ages in the same batch

stress.description: a full 100-passenger batch with random genders and ages spanning both sides of the threshold
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = min(max(1, size), 100)
    details = []
    for i in range(n):
        phone = f'{i:010d}'
        gender = rng.choice(['M', 'F', 'O'])
        age = rng.randint(0, 99)
        seat = f'{i:02d}'
        details.append(f'{phone}{gender}{age:02d}{seat}')
    return [details]
